fields.description:
## Why
The coach's move catalog must offer the right remediation. Two dso lessons need moves: pre-existing issues
should become follow-on tickets (pairs with rebar's discovered_from), and change-detector tests must be
DELETED, not re-pinned to new source text.

## What
Add two Pass-4 coach moves to `MOVE_REGISTRY` in `src/rebar/llm/code_review/moves.py` (and thus
`.rebar/code_review_moves.json`-overridable): `file-follow-on-ticket` (subject = the pre-existing/
out-of-scope issue; template guidance to file a `discovered_from` follow-on ticket) and `delete-bad-test`
(subject = the change-detector/tautological test; template guidance to DELETE it, not re-pin it to the new
source text). Deterministic `{subject}` templates; the LLM only picks the move name + names the subject.

### applies_when gating â€” DATA-FLOW (resolves plan-review G6 block f6f98d71)
The `applies_when` vocabulary is the CLOSED set `{OVERLAY_IDS âˆª "always"}` (`review_kernel.validate_move_registry`),
and a move is offered by Pass-4 ONLY when its `applies_when` overlaps `active_triggers`, where
`active_triggers = {c for f in surfaced for c in f.get("criteria", [])}` (`workflow_ops.py`
`code_review_coach_inputs` / `code_review_coach`, ~line 342). Those `criteria` values are the overlay ids
the reviewers emit (OVERLAY_IDS). Therefore a NOVEL tag (e.g. `"pre-existing"` / `"change-detector"`) would
NEVER enter `active_triggers`, so a move keyed on it would register but never fire (dead move). We
DELIBERATELY DO NOT extend the vocabulary. Instead we key on EXISTING overlay ids already emitted as
`criteria`:
- `delete-bad-test` â†’ `applies_when: ["tests"]`. A change-detector/tautological/over-mocking test finding
  arrives under the `tests` overlay criterion, so the move is offered whenever a surviving `tests` finding
  exists. Its `name`/`template` make it the obvious pick for a change-detector finding (vs re-pinning), and
  it sits alongside the existing `add-regression-test` move so the LLM selects delete â€” not "improve the
  test" â€” for a change-detector subject.
- `file-follow-on-ticket` â†’ `applies_when: ["always"]`. A pre-existing/out-of-scope finding can surface
  under ANY overlay criterion, so the move is always offered; the LLM picks it when the surviving finding
  is about a pre-existing issue (subject = that issue), and the template instructs filing a
  `discovered_from` ticket rather than fixing it in-scope.

No new `applies_when` vocabulary value is introduced; both moves key on values already in the closed set,
so both pass `validate_move_registry` subject-validation and actually enter `active_triggers`.

## Scope
`src/rebar/llm/code_review/moves.py` (MOVE_REGISTRY entries) + a unit test. No verdict-path change; the
coach prompt (`code-review-coach.md`) already picks the single most useful applicable move, so no prompt
change is required beyond the moves being offered.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `file-follow-on-ticket` and `delete-bad-test` moves exist in `MOVE_REGISTRY` with deterministic
      `{subject}` templates and pass `validate_move_registry` (built-in, strict).
- [ ] Each move's `applies_when` uses ONLY existing `{OVERLAY_IDS âˆª "always"}` values (`delete-bad-test` â†’
      `["tests"]`, `file-follow-on-ticket` â†’ `["always"]`), so the move actually enters `active_triggers`
      and is OFFERED by `code_review_coach_inputs` for the intended criteria (proven by a unit test using
      `review_kernel.applicable_moves`).
- [ ] For a surviving finding tagged `criteria: ["tests"]`, `delete-bad-test` is among the applicable moves
      (the change-detector remediation), distinct from the "improve/add test" moves; a unit test pins this.
